2026-04-09T22:11:07.0145119Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-09T22:11:07.0145534Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-09T22:11:07.0145906Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-09T22:11:07.0146210Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m

Without them, I won't be able to perform any GitHub writes. I'm scanning through the available tools and confirming that none of the safe-output functions like add_comment, close_issue, or add_labels are actually present—they'd need to come from an MCP server that isn't connected here. Since the system requires calling at least one of these tools before I can finish, but they're genuinely unavailable in this environment, I'm at an impasse. I'll need to document what I've found and explain this technical constraint.

2026-04-09T22:13:43.6527569Z ◆ The safeoutputs MCP tools are not available in the current execution environment (not present in the function registry). I have completed the analysis but cannot execute GitHub write operations.

Target: *.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"issue_number":{"description":"Issue number to close.
